q.en: What do the Olympic motto words Citius, Altius, Fortius mean?
q.zh: 奥林匹克格言 Citius、Altius、Fortius 是什么意思？

options.en: Peace, Love, Joy
Run, Jump, Throw
Gold, Silver, Bronze according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 金、银、铜（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Faster, Higher, Stronger

explain.en: The book says these three Latin words mean 'Faster, Higher, Stronger' and are the Olympic motto.
explain.zh: 书中说这三个拉丁词意为“更快、更高、更强”，是奥林匹克的格言。

q.en: When and where were the first ancient Olympic Games held?
q.zh: 古代奥林匹克运动会最早于何时何地举行？

options.en: In 2008 in China
In 776 BC in Olympia, Greece
options.zh: 公元前776年，希腊奥林匹亚

options.en: In 1924 in France according to some versions of the story
In 1896 in Athens

explain.en: The book says ancient writings place the first Olympics in Olympia, Greece, in 776 BC (though historians think earlier).
explain.zh: 书中说古代记载把首届奥运会定于公元前776年的希腊奥林匹亚（尽管历史学家认为可能更早）。

q.en: What did the Olympic truce guarantee during the ancient Games?
q.zh: 在古代奥运会期间，奥林匹克休战保证了什么？

options.en: That only kings could attend
That no fighting took place between Greek city-states
options.zh: 希腊各城邦之间不发生战争

options.en: That athletes could not compete
That all wars would end forever according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 所有战争永远结束（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says a strictly enforced truce guaranteed no fighting between any Greek city-states during the Games.
explain.zh: 书中说，一项严格执行的休战保证在奥运会期间希腊各城邦之间不发生任何战争。

q.en: What do the five interlocking rings on the Olympic flag represent?
q.zh: 奥林匹克旗帜上五个相连的圆环代表什么？

options.en: The five settled continents working together in peace
options.zh: 五大定居大陆在和平中团结协作

options.en: The five colors of the rainbow according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 彩虹的五种颜色（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The five largest countries
The five Olympic sports

explain.en: The book says the 1920 flag's interlocking rings represent the five settled continents of the world working together in peace.
explain.zh: 书中说，1920年启用的旗帜上五环代表世界上五大定居大陆在和平中团结协作。

q.en: Who founded the modern International Olympic Committee (IOC) in 1894?
q.zh: 是谁在1894年创立了现代国际奥林匹克委员会（IOC）？

options.en: The Emperor Theodosius
A Greek king
A Frenchman named Pierre de Coubertin
options.zh: 一位名叫皮埃尔·德·顾拜旦的法国人

options.en: An American runner named Jesse Owens according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位名叫杰西·欧文斯的美国赛跑选手（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Pierre de Coubertin formed the International Olympic Committee, or IOC, in 1894.
explain.zh: 书中说皮埃尔·德·顾拜旦于1894年创立了国际奥林匹克委员会（IOC）。

q.en: Why does the book mention Jesse Owens at the 1936 Berlin Olympics?
q.zh: 本书为什么要提到1936年柏林奥运会的杰西·欧文斯？

options.en: To explain how to light the torch according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了解释如何点燃火炬（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To show how an African American athlete upset Nazi racist plans
options.zh: 为了展示一位非裔美国运动员如何挫败了纳粹的种族主义图谋

options.en: To describe a swimming race
options.zh: 为了描述一场游泳比赛

options.en: To list the host cities

explain.en: The book uses Owens's gold-medal performance to show how an African American upset the Nazis' plans to prove white superiority.
explain.zh: 书中用欧文斯的金牌表现，说明一位非裔美国人如何挫败了纳粹宣扬白人优越的图谋。

q.en: How is the Olympic torch first lit for the relay?
q.zh: 奥林匹克火炬在接力中最初是如何点燃的？

options.en: In Olympia using magnified sunlight, then carried by runners
options.zh: 在奥林匹亚用放大的阳光点燃，再由跑步者传递

options.en: By a candle at the North Pole
From a bonfire in France
options.zh: 从法国的一堆篝火点燃

options.en: With a match in the host city according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在主办城市用火柴点燃（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the torch is lit in Olympia using magnified sunlight and runners carry it to the host city.
explain.zh: 书中说火炬在奥林匹亚用放大的阳光点燃，由跑步者一路传递到主办城市。

q.en: When were women first officially allowed to compete in the modern Olympics?
q.zh: 妇女最早是在何时被正式允许参加现代奥运会的？

options.en: In 1936 in Berlin
In 2008 in Beijing
At the 1928 Summer Games in Amsterdam
At the very first Games in 1896 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在1896年首届奥运会上（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says women were officially allowed to compete for the first time at the 1928 Summer Games in Amsterdam.
explain.zh: 书中说，妇女首次被正式允许参赛是在1928年阿姆斯特丹夏季奥运会上。

q.en: After 1992, how did the schedule of the Summer and Winter Games change?
q.zh: 1992年之后，夏季与冬季奥运会的安排发生了什么变化？

options.en: They are held in different years, two years apart
options.zh: 它们在不同的年份举行，相隔两年

options.en: They are held in the same year according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们在同一年举行（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They happen every ten years
They stopped being held

explain.en: The book says IOC officials decided after 1992 to hold the Summer and Winter Games in different years, two years apart.
explain.zh: 书中说，国际奥委会在1992年后决定让夏季与冬季奥运会在不同年份、相隔两年举行。

q.en: According to the book, what do the modern Games continue to represent?
q.zh: 根据本书，现代奥运会继续代表着什么？

options.en: A way to make the most money
The power of the strongest army
A contest of who builds the biggest stadium according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 比谁建造的体育场最大（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The ideal of sportsmanship and peaceful competition among nations
options.zh: 体育精神与各国之间和平竞赛的理想

explain.en: The book concludes that, like the ancient Games, the modern Olympics represent sportsmanship and peaceful competition among nations.
explain.zh: 书中总结道，与现代奥运会一样，古代奥运会代表着体育精神与各国之间的和平竞赛。

prompt.en: The Olympics began as a way for city-states to compete in peace. Why do you think peaceful competition between countries still matters today?
prompt.zh: 奥林匹克最初是城邦之间和平竞赛的方式。你认为国家之间的和平竞赛在当今为什么仍然重要？

en: Think about the Olympic truce and the motto 'Faster, Higher, Stronger.'
Give one reason peaceful competition can bring people together.
zh: 想想奥林匹克休战以及格言“更快、更高、更强”。
给出一个和平竞赛能把人们凝聚在一起的理由。
